title: Fermented Crunchy Peruvian Aubergine Gratin
description: A creative dinner recipe that combines the flavors of Peru with the tanginess of fermentation. This dish features roasted aubergine, crunchy fermented corn kernels, and a tangy salsa.

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon Peruvian aji amarillo pepper paste

instructions: Cut the aubergines in half lengthwise and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the olive oil over the aubergines and season with salt and pepper.
Roast the aubergines in the oven for 30-40 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly caramelized.
While the aubergines are roasting, heat a skillet over medium heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the aji amarillo pepper paste, if using, and cook for an additional minute.
Remove the aubergines from the oven and let them cool slightly.
Scoop the flesh of the aubergines out of the skin and place it in a blender or food processor.
Add the fermented corn kernels, queso fresco, and cilantro to the blender.
Blend the mixture until it is smooth and creamy.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and top it with additional grated queso fresco, if desired.
Bake the gratin in the oven for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned and the mixture is heated through.
